DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata presentation rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Example Definition

A unified example model used across DSDS. Each example provides a visual or interactive demonstration via a presentation, a literal value, or both. Optional title, description, and value properties provide context. Presentations are independent typed objects defined in presentation.schema.json for images, videos, code snippets, and URLs. The `example` def is used as an `example[]` array within other guideline items (best-practice examples, variant previews, state previews, interaction illustrations, anatomy previews, motion examples, content label examples, and localization examples).

Source: common/example.schema.json

example

A single example. Every example requires at least a presentation or a value (or both). The presentation provides a visual or interactive demonstration. The value carries a literal value for API property examples. The optional title and description provide context. When only a value is provided without a presentation, the example represents a concrete data point (e.g., a prop value of 'primary' or a token value of 44) — tools SHOULD render these as table rows or inline displays rather than visual demonstrations.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
valueanyat least oneA literal value carried by the example. Used for API property examples where the content is a concrete value rather than a visual demonstration. Accepts any JSON type: string (e.g., 'primary'), number (e.g., 44), boolean (e.g., true), object, array, or null. When provided without a presentation, this value alone constitutes the example. Do not use `value` for display text — use `title` for captions and `description` for explanations.
presentationpresentationImage | presentationVideo | presentationCode | presentationUrlat least oneThe visual or interactive presentation for the example. Required when `value` is not provided. Optional when `value` is provided — value-only examples represent concrete data points (prop values, token values) that tools render as table rows or inline displays.
titlestringoptionalA human-readable caption or title for the example. This is a display label (e.g., 'Primary button in default state'). For API property examples, use `value` for the literal content and `title` for the human-readable label.
descriptionstringoptionalAn explanation of the example — why it is encouraged, discouraged, or notable.

Constraint: At least one of presentation, value must be present.

References: presentationImage, presentationVideo, presentationCode, presentationUrl

Example

[
  {
    "title": "Primary button — default state",
    "description": "The high-emphasis button style used for the most important action on a surface.",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "image",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-primary-default.png",
      "alt": "A primary button with a solid blue background and white label text reading Save."
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "JSX — button group with primary and secondary",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "code",
      "code": "<ButtonGroup>\n  <Button variant=\"secondary\">Cancel</Button>\n  <Button variant=\"primary\">Save</Button>\n</ButtonGroup>",
      "language": "jsx"
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "Loading state transition",
    "description": "Shows the full loading lifecycle from click through completion.",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "video",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/assets/button-loading-interaction.mp4",
      "alt": "A user clicks Save. The label is replaced by a spinner. After two seconds, the spinner becomes a checkmark and the label changes to Saved."
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "Interactive primary button story",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "url",
      "url": "https://storybook.acme.com/?path=/story/components-button--primary"
    }
  },
  {
    "title": "variant property — primary",
    "value": "primary",
    "presentation": {
      "type": "code",
      "code": "<Button variant=\"primary\">Save</Button>",
      "language": "jsx"
    }
  }
]
↑ Back to top
